openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/ser.purpose: Owns the mechanics of usage-based realization runs — rating, run persistence, credit allocation/correction, lineage writes, invoice usage booking, and payment authorization/settlement. Never makes state-machine decisions; executes run operations only.

patterns.name: Config-struct constructor with Validate()
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all four required dependencies (Adapter, Rater, Handler, Lineage) before returning *Service. Config.Validate() uses errors.Join over a []error slice.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } return &Service{...}, nil }

patterns.name: Input structs validate first
patterns.description: Every exported method calls in.Validate() as its first action. Input structs implement Validate() and validate sub-fields recursively with wrapped fmt.Errorf messages.
patterns.example: func (s *Service) CreateRatedRun(ctx context.Context, in CreateRatedRunInput) (...) { if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., err } ... }

patterns.name: CreditAllocationMode enum for credit semantics
patterns.description: Three-valued enum CreditAllocationNone/Exact/Available controls whether credits are allocated and whether exact-match is required. Its own Validate() catches invalid values.
patterns.example: CreditAllocation: CreditAllocationExact // allocates exact run total; error if ledger cannot satisfy

patterns.name: Atomic credit-realization + lineage writes
patterns.description: createRunCreditRealizations always calls lineage.CreateInitialLineages then lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments immediately after adapter.CreateRunCreditRealization. These three calls must never be split.

patterns.name: Handler callbacks for all ledger side-effects
patterns.description: Ledger writes (credit allocation, payment authorization, settlement, invoice usage accrual) go through usagebased.Handler callbacks. Never call ledger adapters directly from this package.

patterns.name: Round before compare or persist
patterns.description: Decimals must be rounded via CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision before negative-total checks, delta comparisons, and UpdateRealizationRun calls. Never compare unrounded alpacadecimal.Decimal values.

patterns.name: NoFiatTransactionRequired short-circuit
patterns.description: BookInvoicedPaymentAuthorized and SettleInvoicedPayment both short-circuit with a no-op result when in.Run.NoFiatTransactionRequired is true, skipping handler and adapter calls.
patterns.example: if in.Run.NoFiatTransactionRequired { return BookInvoicedPaymentAuthorizedResult{Run: in.Run}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service struct, Config, New constructor, and CreditAllocationMode enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No state-machine logic ever belongs here — this package executes runs, it does not decide which state to enter.

key_file_guides.role: CreateRatedRun: full run creation — rate, validate totals, persist run+detailed lines, optionally allocate credits, update run totals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: runTotals must be rounded before the negative-total check and before UpdateRealizationRun. ensureDetailedLinesLoadedForRating must be called before rating to satisfy PriorRuns validation.

key_file_guides.role: ReconcileCredits (delta allocation/correction) and CorrectAllCredits (zero out run credits).
key_file_guides.watch_for: TargetAmount and currentAmount must both be rounded before computing delta — comparing unrounded decimals produces incorrect billing corrections.

key_file_guides.role: Private allocate() helper and createRunCreditRealizations() that pairs adapter credit creation with lineage writes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call adapter.CreateRunCreditRealization without the two subsequent lineage calls — they must be treated as a single atomic business operation.

key_file_guides.role: BookAccruedInvoiceUsage: books invoice usage accrual via handler then persists AccruedUsage. Short-circuits on NoFiatTransactionRequired.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Empty TransactionGroupID returned from handler is an error — enforce this check before calling adapter.CreateRunInvoicedUsage.

key_file_guides.role: BookInvoicedPaymentAuthorized and SettleInvoicedPayment: authorize/settle payment via handler then persist via adapter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: State guards in Validate() (payment already authorized, mismatched line ID, already settled) must be preserved for any new payment methods.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for BookInvoicedPaymentAuthorizedInput.Validate() and SettleInvoicedPaymentInput.Validate() covering all rejection paths.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Test helpers (newUsageBasedCharge, newUsageBasedRun) construct values directly — do not extract them to testutils; they depend on internal types.

anti_patterns: Making state-machine decisions (status transitions, trigger selection) inside this package — those belong in the usagebased charge service layer above.
Calling ledger adapters directly — all ledger side-effects must flow through usagebased.Handler callbacks.
Splitting createRunCreditRealizations from its lineage calls — CreateInitialLineages and PersistCorrectionLineageSegments must always follow credit creation atomically.
Comparing alpacadecimal.Decimal values before rounding with CurrencyCalculator — rounding order is required for billing correctness.
Assigning charge.Intent.TaxConfig pointer directly into DetailedLine — always clone with cfg.Clone() to prevent mutation across run boundaries.

decisions.decision: Service owns run mechanics but not state-machine decisions
decisions.rationale: Separating 'how to execute a run' (rating, persistence, credit math) from 'when to execute a run' (state transitions) keeps this package testable without a full state machine.

decisions.decision: Handler interface for all ledger side-effects
decisions.rationale: Ledger calls are externally provided via usagebased.Handler so this package compiles and tests without a real ledger implementation; the integration point is a single seam per operation type.

decisions.decision: CreditAllocationMode enum instead of boolean flags
decisions.rationale: Three distinct allocation semantics (none, exact, available) cannot be expressed cleanly with a boolean; an enum with Validate() catches invalid values at construction time.

code_examples.scenario: Create a final realization run with exact credit allocation
